channel.title: Comments for page "The Dragon and the Pesterbot"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Dragon and the Pesterbot"

item.title: Re: ALL SHALL BOW BEFORE THE DILDOTRON
item.content:encoded: I bet it was Bright who gave him that name!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They were made for this crossover. It's ridiculous and great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm all for spreading it around. I appreciate the reading. I like the way you did the voices. I have to digitally edit the track to even attempt 1370s voice.

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: That's an epic reading. Much appreciated. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my reading version https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qdgljkwDtlA&t

theeSherms is better but in the mean time hit up SCP redings, hit up East side SCP show, Hit up ordinarymenwhofightlikeheros, Volgun, livingnightmre…. lets see if we cant get this gem sped across the SCP readers multiverse. …. And go give the Sherm a like and a sub.. he makes great stuff. he did duke till dawn and SCP 3999 he has earned his accolaids…… and give my channel a look to because why not and give this story a +1 because it was awesome.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
It made me laugh, and I absolutely love doing Pesterbot voice. Here's a Skip-Tape!

https://youtu.be/Rty6iBgg6Zs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it, though I gotta wonder who taught Pesterbot that last one. Was cool to see something with the spirit of the old stories be written now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… As a young spawn, I was told tales of such… greatness. It seemed unfathomable then, only a remnant of the past universes; the ones to outshine us. They were the universes of kings and legends. But, I digress. This is why I was born… to see PesterBot and Gargantua The Dinosaur have a petty argument.

+1, my generous traveler.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded: No objections here. In fact I'll go ahead and make those edits now rather than waiting for the clock to run out and making you do it.

item.title: Re: ALL SHALL BOW BEFORE THE DILDOTRON
item.content:encoded: Dildo's can have dreams too, even if they aren't reaalllyyy dildos and instead are just an annoying robotic thing. (+1)

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made by author.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please PM a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The proposed crosslinks are to link this tale to the SCPs that it references, which are SCP-682 and SCP-1370 - both of which are the primary foci of this tale.

Editing this:

With the second, an enormous lizard burst through it, sending a glass display case flying as it shrugged off the copper jacketed lead that impacted its back.

A tinny, high-pitched voice rang out in the room. "Who dares to disturb the Prime Minister Sinister? I shall rip your eyes from their sockets and force you to eat them!"

To this:

With the second, an enormous lizard burst through it, sending a glass display case flying as it shrugged off the copper jacketed lead that impacted its back.

A tinny, high-pitched voice rang out in the room. "Who dares to disturb the Prime Minister Sinister? I shall rip your eyes from their sockets and force you to eat them!"

item.title: ALL SHALL BOW BEFORE THE DILDOTRON
item.content:encoded:
"Dildotron shall destroy you too!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made me smile. A well deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was cute and funny, and definitely worth reading.

+1

pesterbot best bot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You say that like it's a bad thing At least we'll always have Resurrection. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet. The last exchange made me giggle. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
also no one writes 682 like this anymore

Statements like these are just begging for the flood of 682 fanfics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, to add to that, no one's written about Pesterbot lately that I can recall, and also no one writes 682 like this anymore, so it's a fun bit of nostalgia. >:B So there!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no one ever writes about Pesterbot

There are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand, there's not much to this. On the other, no one ever writes about Pesterbot, and that is a shame. Plus, it's really funny. :D

At the last moment it lost its balance awkwardly toppled over backwards and rolled up against the wall.

Comma after 'moment', but more importantly, 'it' is not clear in this sentence, since you're referring to both of them that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It made me smile, and I read the thing, so it gets a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cute enough for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the idea of 1370 challenging 682 stuck in my head and shook it around until this came out. Seriously though, I had to write this. It would have bugged me for weeks otherwise. I had a lot of fun with it. Hope you all enjoy reading it as much as I enjoyed writing it.
